name: ebook2text

metadata.name: ebook2text
metadata.summary: Convert common book file types to text for machine learning

documentation:
# Convert Ebook File

## Overview

This Python script provides functionality for converting various ebook file formats (EPUB, DOCX, PDF, TXT) into a standardized text format. The script processes each file, identifying chapters, and replaces chapter headers with asterisks. It also performs OCR (Optical Character Recognition) for image-based text using GPT-4o and standardizes the text by desmartening punctuation.

## Features

- **File Format Support**: Handles EPUB, DOCX, PDF, and TXT formats.
- **Chapter Identification**: Detects and marks chapter breaks.
- **OCR Capability**: Converts text from images using OCR.
- **Text Standardization**: Replaces smart punctuation with ASCII equivalents.

## Requirements

To run this script, you need Python 3.9 or above and the following packages:

- `python-docx`
- `openai`
- `python-dotenv`
- `bs4`
- `pdfminer.six`
- `pillow`

## Usage

1. Ensure all dependencies are installed.
2. Set your environment variable for the OpenAI API key.
3. Place your ebook files in a known directory.
4. Run the script with the path to the ebook file and a metadata dictionary with keys of 'title' and 'author' as arguments.

- set `save_file` to False, if you want a string returned.
- provide a Path object of a file name to be written to, to use a custom output filename.

## Functions

- `convert_file(file_path: Path, metadata: dict, *, save_file: bool = True, save_path: Optional[Path] = None) -> Union[str, None]`: Main function to convert an ebook file to text.

## Contributing

Contributions to this project are welcome. Please use Ruff for formatting to ensure that your code follows the existing style for consistency.

## License

This project is licensed by ProsePal LLC under the MIT license

## Version History

- **v0.1.0** (Release date: November 30, 2023)
  - Initial release

- **v0.1.1** (Release date: December 2, 2023)
  - fixed false positives for is_number

- **v0.2.0** (Release date: December 3, 2023)
  - Conversion of docx files

- **v0.3.0** (Release date: December 8, 2023)
  - Conversion of PDF files

- **v0.3.1** (Release date: January 23, 2024)
  - fixed concatenation of text in pdf conversion
  - updated pillow version to secure version

- **v1.0.0** (Release date: January 23, 2024)
  - created library instead of single module

- **v1.0.1** (Release date: March 13, 2024)
  - setup.py and requirements.txt typo fixed

- **v1.0.2** (Release date: May 17, 2024)
  - added tests, fixed minor typos

- **v1.1.0** (Release date: May 30, 2024)
  - Change to abstract factory pattern

- **v1.1.1** (Release date: May 31, 2024)
  - Pull current version of ebooklib from Github and folded it into library since package repo out of date

- **v1.1.2** (Release date: May 31, 2024)
  - FIX: Put ebooklib in correct directory.

- **v1.1.3** (Release date: October 27, 2024)
  - FIX: Initialize logging

- **v1.1.4** (Release date: November 7, 2024)
  - YANKED

- **v1.1.5** (Release date: November 7, 2024)
  - FIX: Move logging to own module

- **v1.1.6** (Release date: November 9, 2024)
  - FIX: Catch PDFSyntaxError and empty image lists, small performance improvement to run_ocr

- **v1.1.7** (Release date November 10, 2024)
  - FIX: Line concatenation issue in PDFs

- **v2.0.0** (Release date December 4, 2024)
  - REFACTOR: Converters are now packages with more streamlined constructors.
  - BREAKING FEATURE: ebook2text now takes Path objects instead of string filenames.
  - BREAKING FEATURE: Converters no longer have a ChapterSplit class. This is handled by the BookConversion class, with no more circular imports.
  - NEW FEATURE: `convert_file` now has optional `save_file` and `save_path` arguments to allow for custom output filenames or for a string to be returned instead.

functions.name: ebook2text.epub_conversion.convert_epub
documentation:
A convenience function that reads an EPUB file and splits it into
chapters.

Args:
    file_path (str): The path to the EPUB file to be read.
    metadata (dict): A dictionary containing metadata such as title and
        author information.

Returns:
    str: The cleaned text of the chapters separated by '***'.

Converts EPUB files to text and splits chapters.

This class extends the BookConversion abstract class and provides methods
for reading EPUB files,extracting text from elements, extracting images,
processing chapter text, and splitting chapters.

Args:
    file_path (str): The path to the EPUB file to be read.
    metadata (dict): A dictionary containing metadata such as title and
        author information.

functions.name: ebook2text.pdf_conversion.convert_pdf
documentation:
A convenience function that reads a PDF file and splits its content into
chapters based on chapter boundaries.
This function initializes a PDFConverter object with the provided file
path and metadata. It then calls the 'split_chapters' method of the
PDFConverter instance to extract text from the PDF, identify chapter
boundaries, and split the content into chapters. The resulting text,
representing the split chapters, is returned as a single string.
Args:
    file_path (str): The path to the PDF file to be read.
    metadata (dict): A dictionary containing metadata such as title and
        author information.

Returns:
    str: The content of the PDF file split into chapters based on chapter
        boundaries.

classes.name: ebook2text.pdf_conversion.pdf_text_extractor.PDFTextExtractor

methods.name: ebook2text.pdf_conversion.pdf_text_extractor.PDFTextExtractor._extract_element_data
documentation:
Extracts text and image data from a PDF page.

This function receives an LTPage object extracted by PDFMiner and returns
a tuple containing a list of image object numbers and a list of strings
containing the text from the page.

Args:pdf
    page (LTPage): The page to extract text and image data from.

Returns:
    tuple[list[int], list[str]]: A tuple containing a list of image object
        numbers and a list of strings containing the text from the page.

methods.name: ebook2text.pdf_conversion.pdf_text_extractor.PDFTextExtractor.extract_text
documentation:
Extracts text from a PDF page, including OCR text from images.

This method processes a given PDF page represented by the LTPage
object. It iterates through the elements on the page, categorizes them
as images or text using the '_process_element' method, and collects
the object numbers of images and text content. For text elements, it
appends the extracted text to a list after checking for the presence
of actual text. If the element is an image, it adds the object number
to a separate list. After processing all elements, it calls the
'_extract_text_from_image' method to extract text from images using
OCR. Finally, it combines the OCR text with the extracted text content
from the page and returns the concatenated result as a single string.

Args:
    page (LTPage): The PDF page represented as an LTPage object.

Returns:
    List[str]: The extracted text content from the PDF page, including OCR
        text from images.

methods.name: ebook2text.pdf_conversion.pdf_text_extractor.PDFTextExtractor._process_element
documentation:
Processes a PDF layout element to identify its type and extract
relevant data.

This function categorizes a given PDF layout element into types such
as image, text, or other. For image elements, it returns the object
ID. For text elements, it returns the extracted text. The function
handles container elements by recursively processing their children.
If an element does not match any specific type, it is categorized as
"other".

Args:
    element (Any): A PDF layout element from pdfminer.

Returns:
    Tuple[str, Union[int, str]]: A tuple containing the element
        type and its relevant data (object ID for images or text
        content for text elements), or None for other types.

methods.name: ebook2text.pdf_conversion.pdf_text_extractor.PDFTextExtractor._extract_image_text
documentation: Collect list of Base64 encoded images and run them through OCR

The PDFTextExtractor class implements the functionality to extract text
from a PDF page, including OCR text from images.

Methods:
    extract_text: Extracts text from a PDF page, including OCR text from
        images.

functions.name: ebook2text.pdf_conversion.pdf_line_logic.is_header
documentation:
Determines if the line is part of the page header by checking if it starts
or ends with the title or author.

functions.name: ebook2text.pdf_conversion.pdf_line_logic.check_line
documentation:
Check if the line indicates something special should be done.

This function calls is_header(), is_chapter(), and is_not_chapter() to
determine if the line is a chapter marker, the marker of a page that is
not a chapter - such as frontmatter or backmatter -, part of the page
header, or a regular line.

Args:
    line (str): The line to check.
    metadata (dict[str, str]): The metadata dictionary.
    header_detected (bool): Whether the header has already been detected.

Returns:
    tuple[LineType, bool]: A tuple containing an enum of line type and a
        boolean indicating whether the header has been detected.

functions.name: ebook2text.pdf_conversion.pdf_line_logic.compare_lines
documentation:
Compare two line types and determine what action should be taken.

Args:
    previous_line (LineType): The previous line type.
    current_line (LineType): The current line type.
    last_action (LineAction): The last action taken.

Returns:
    LineAction: An enum of the action to take.

methods.name: ebook2text.pdf_conversion.pdf_converter.PDFConverter.return_string
documentation: Return the parsed text as a string.

PDFConverter class for converting PDF files to text and images.

This class provides methods for extracting text and images from PDF files.
It includes functions for processing PDF layout elements, extracting
images in base64 format, running OCR on images, splitting text into
paragraphs, and checking for chapter boundaries.

Args:
    file_path (str): Path to the PDF file.
    metadata (dict): Dictionary with title and author name.

functions.name: ebook2text.pdf_conversion.pdf_image_extractor._expand_bits
documentation: Convert 2 or 4 bit data to 8 bit

functions.name: ebook2text.pdf_conversion.pdf_image_extractor._get_pillow_mode
documentation:
Get the Pillow mode based on bit depth and color space.

Args:
    color_space (str): Color space of the image.

Returns:
    str: Pillow mode.

methods.name: ebook2text.pdf_conversion.pdf_image_extractor.PDFImageExtractor.extract_images
documentation:
Processes and extracts images from PDF objects based on the provided
object numbers.

This method reads the PDF file, initializes a PDF parser, and iterates
through the list of object numbers. For each object number, it
attempts to retrieve the image data using the '_get_image' method. If
successful, the binary image data is converted into a base64-encoded
JPEG format string and added to the list. The final output is a list
of base64-encoded strings representing the images extracted from the
PDF.

Args:
    obj_nums (List[int]): A list of object numbers corresponding to
    images in the PDF.

Returns:
    List[str]: A list of base64-encoded strings representing the
        extracted images.

methods.name: ebook2text.pdf_conversion.pdf_image_extractor.PDFImageExtractor._create_image_from_binary
documentation:
Convert binary image data into a base64-encoded JPEG string.

Args:
    stream (bytes): The binary data of the image.
    width (int): The width of the image in pixels.
    height (int): The height of the image in pixels.

Returns:
    str: A base64-encoded string representing the PNG image.

Raises:
    Exception: If there is an error in processing the image data.

methods.name: ebook2text.pdf_conversion.pdf_image_extractor.PDFImageExtractor._transcode_to_png
documentation:
Converts a JPEG byte stream to a PNG-based base64-encoded string
without decoding.

Args:
    jpeg_data (bytes): Byte stream of the JPEG image.

Returns:
    bytes: PNG byte stream.

methods.name: ebook2text.pdf_conversion.pdf_image_extractor.PDFImageExtractor._get_image
documentation:
Processes and retrieves images from a PDF object.

Args:
    obj_num (int): The object number of the PDF image to retrieve.
    attempt (Optional[int]): The number of attempts made to retrieve
        the image (default is 0).

Returns:
    str: A base64-encoded string representing the JPEG image if
        successful, otherwise an empty string.

Raises:
    Exception: If there is an error in processing the image data or if
        the image does not meet the required criteria.

methods.name: ebook2text.pdf_conversion.pdf_image_extractor.PDFImageExtractor._find_next_image
documentation:
Finds the next image object in the PDF document.

Args:
    obj_num (int): The object number of the current image.

Returns:
    int: The object number of the next image in the PDF document.

Set the logger for the entire library.

functions.name: ebook2text.text_utilities.desmarten_text
documentation:
Replace smart punctuation in the given text with regular ASCII
punctuation.
Args:
    book_content (str): The input text containing smart punctuation.
Returns:
    (str) The text with smart punctuation replaced by regular punctuation.

functions.name: ebook2text.text_utilities.clean_chapter_breaks
documentation:
Replace chapter breaks in the given text with a single chapter break.
Args:
    full_text: The input text containing chapter breaks.
    chapter_break: The string representing a chapter break.
Returns:
    The text with chapter breaks replaced by a single chapter break.

functions.name: ebook2text.text_utilities.remove_leading_chapter_breaks
documentation:
Remove leading chapter breaks from the given text.
Args:
    full_text: The input text containing leading chapter breaks.
Returns:
    The text with leading chapter breaks removed.

functions.name: ebook2text.text_utilities.remove_whitespace
documentation:
Remove extra whitespace from the given text.
Args:
    full_text: The input text with extra whitespace.
Returns:
    The text with extra whitespace removed.

functions.name: ebook2text.text_utilities.clean_text
documentation:
Clean the given text by removing leading chapter breaks, chapter breaks,
and extra whitespace.
Args:
    full_text: The input text to be cleaned.
Returns:
    The cleaned text.

Parses the book lines, replacing chapter headers with asterisks and

Yields a line-by-line of the processed book content as a string.

Perform optical character recognition (OCR) on a list of base64-encoded
images using the OpenAI API.

Arguments:
    base64_images (list): A list of base64-encoded images.

Returns str: The recognized text from the images.

Custom exception class for handling errors related to an image being too

documentation:
Custom exception class for handling errors related to an image being too

functions.name: ebook2text.convert_file._initialize_converter
documentation:
Initialize the appropriate converter based on the file extension.

Args:
    file_path (Path): The path to the book file.
    metadata (dict): Dictionary with title and author name.
    extension (str): The file extension of the book file.

Returns:
    Union[BookConversion, TextParser]: The initialized converter.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the file type is not supported.

functions.name: ebook2text.convert_file._parse_file_path
documentation: Create the text file name from the book file name.

functions.name: ebook2text.convert_file.convert_file
documentation:
Converts a book to a text file with 3 asterisks for chapter breaks
Args:
    file_path: Path to the book file.
    metadata: Dictionary with title and author name.
    save_file: Boolean to save the file or not.
    save_path: (Optional) Path to save the file to.

Returns:
    None if save_file is False, else returns the parsed text as a string.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the file type is not supported (inherited from
        _initialize_converter).

functions.name: ebook2text.chapter_check.roman_to_int
documentation:
Convert a Roman numeral to an integer.
Arguments:
    roman_num (str): A string representing the Roman numeral.
Returns int: The integer value of the Roman numeral.

functions.name: ebook2text.chapter_check.word_to_num
documentation:
Convert a spelled-out number without spaces or hyphens to a string of the
integer. The function supports numbers from 0 to 99 and is case
insensitive.
Args:
    number_string (str): A string representing the spelled-out number.
Returns:
    int: The integer value of the spelled-out number.

functions.name: ebook2text.chapter_check.is_spelled_out_number
documentation:
Try to convert the word to an integer. If it's not a valid spelled-out
number, it will raise a ValueError

functions.name: ebook2text.chapter_check.is_roman_numeral
documentation:
Try to convert the word to an integer. If it"s not a valid roman numeral,
it will raise a ValueError

functions.name: ebook2text.chapter_check.is_number
documentation:
Check if a string contains a number value either as digits,
roman numerals, or spelled-out numbers.

functions.name: ebook2text.chapter_check.is_chapter
documentation:
Check if a string contains the word "chapter", a Roman numeral, a
spelled-out number, or a digit.
Arguments:
    s (str): The string to check.
Returns bool: True if the string meets the criteria, False otherwise.

functions.name: ebook2text.chapter_check.is_not_chapter
documentation: Checks if the given line is not a chapter.

classes.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_text_extractor.DocxTextExtractor

methods.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_text_extractor.DocxTextExtractor.extract_text
documentation:
Extracts the text content from the paragraph, performs OCR on any
images present, and returns whichever is not empty.

Args:
    paragraph: The Paragraph object containing the text and formatting.

Returns:
    str: The extracted and processed text.

methods.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_text_extractor.DocxTextExtractor._extract_image_text
documentation: Extracts text from images within the paragraph using OCR.
Class dedicated to extracting and processing text from docx Paragraphs.

functions.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.convert_docx
documentation:
A convenience function that reads the contents of a DOCX file and returns
the processed text.

Args:
    file_path (str): The path to the DOCX file.
    metadata (dict): Metadata about the document.

Returns:
    str: The processed text of the DOCX file formatted into chapters.

classes.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_converter.DocxConverter

methods.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_converter.DocxConverter.__init__
documentation: Initializes the DocxConverter with file path and metadata.

methods.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_converter.DocxConverter._read_file
documentation:
Reads a Word document from the specified file path.

Returns:
    List[Paragraph]: A list of paragraph objects extracted from the
        Word document object.

methods.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_converter.DocxConverter.parse_file
documentation:
Process paragraphs to organize them into pages and chapters, handling
page breaks and chapter starts, and compile the final structured text
of the book.

Returns:
    str: The structured text of the entire book.

methods.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_converter.DocxConverter.write_text
documentation:
Write the parsed text to a file.

Args:
    text (str): The parsed text to be written to the file.

methods.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_converter.DocxConverter.return_string
documentation: Return the parsed text as a string.

methods.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_converter.DocxConverter._remove_smart_punctuation
documentation:
Remove smart punctuation from the given text.

Args:
    text (str): The text to have smart punctuation removed.

Returns:
    str: The text with smart punctuation removed.

methods.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_converter.DocxConverter._contains_page_break
documentation:
Checks if a given paragraph contains a page break.
Args:
    paragraph: The Paragraph object containing the text and formatting
Returns:
    bool: True if the paragraph contains a page break, False otherwise

methods.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_converter.DocxConverter._check_index
documentation:
Checks if the given index exceeds a predefined maximum limit for lines
to check.

Args:
    index (int): The index to be checked against the maximum limit.

Returns:
    bool: True if the index is greater than or equal to the maximum
        limit, False otherwise.

methods.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_converter.DocxConverter._is_start_of_chapter
documentation:
Checks if the given text marks the start of a new chapter based on
specific criteria.

Args:
    text (str): The text to be checked for chapter header indicators.
    index (int): The number of paragraphs since the last page break.

Returns:
    bool: True if the text is considered the start of a chapter, False
        otherwise.

methods.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_converter.DocxConverter._is_non_chapter
documentation:
Checks if the given text is not a chapter based on specific criteria.

Args:
    text (str): The text to be checked for being front or back matter.
    index (int): The paragraph index of the text within the chapter.

Returns:
    bool: True if the text is not a chapter, False otherwise.

methods.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_converter.DocxConverter._process_text
documentation:
Process a paragraph's text to determine if it starts a new chapter and
format it accordingly.

Args:
    paragraph_text (str): The text of the paragraph to be processed.
    current_para_index (int): The index of the paragraph
        within the current chapter.

Returns:
    Tuple[str, int]: A tuple containing the processed text and the
        updated index of the paragraph within its chapter.

Class to convert a Word document to structured text.

Attributes:
    file_path (str): The path to the Word document.
    metadata (dict): Metadata related to the document.
    doc (Document): The parsed Word document object.
    paragraphs (list): List of paragraphs objects extracted from the
        document.

classes.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_image_extractor.DocxImageExtractor

methods.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_image_extractor.DocxImageExtractor.extract_images
documentation:
Extracts and converts images found in the paragraph into
base64-encoded strings.

Returns:
    list: A list of base64-encoded strings, each representing an image
        extracted from the paragraph.

methods.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_image_extractor.DocxImageExtractor._build_base64_images_list
documentation:
Converts image blobs to base64-encoded strings.

Args:
    image_blobs (list): A list of extracted image streams.

Returns:
    list: A list of base64-encoded strings representing the images.

methods.name: ebook2text.docx_conversion.docx_image_extractor.DocxImageExtractor._extract_image_blobs
documentation:
Extracts image blobs from the paragraph.

Returns:
    list: A list of image blobs extracted from the paragraph.
A class dedicated to extracting images from docx Paragraph objects.

functions.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.write_epub
documentation:
Creates epub file with the content defined in EpubBook.

```pycon
>>> ebooklib.write_epub('book.epub', book)
```

```
Args

name: file name for the output file

book: instance of EpubBook

options: extra opions as dictionary (optional)
```
WRITE

functions.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.read_epub
documentation:
Creates new instance of EpubBook with the content defined in the input file.

```pycon
>>> book = ebooklib.read_epub('book.epub')
```

```
Args

name: full path to the input file

options: extra options as dictionary (optional)

Returns

Instance of EpubBook.
```
READ

classes.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.Section
documentation: TOC and navigation elements

classes.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubException
documentation: Exceptions

classes.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubItem

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubItem.__init__
documentation:
:Args:
  - uid: Unique identifier for this item (optional)
  - file_name: File name for this item (optional)
  - media_type: Media type for this item (optional)
  - content: Content for this item (optional)
  - manifest: Manifest for this item (optional)

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubItem.get_id
documentation:
Returns unique identifier for this item.

:Returns:
  Returns uid number as string.

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubItem.get_name
documentation:
Returns name for this item. By default it is always file name but it does not have to be.

:Returns:
  Returns file name for this item.

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubItem.get_type
documentation:
Guess type according to the file extension. Might not be the best way how to do it, but it works for now.

Items can be of type:
  - ITEM_UNKNOWN = 0
  - ITEM_IMAGE = 1
  - ITEM_STYLE = 2
  - ITEM_SCRIPT = 3
  - ITEM_NAVIGATION = 4
  - ITEM_VECTOR = 5
  - ITEM_FONT = 6
  - ITEM_VIDEO = 7
  - ITEM_AUDIO = 8
  - ITEM_DOCUMENT = 9
  - ITEM_COVER = 10

We map type according to the extensions which are defined in ebooklib.EXTENSIONS.

:Returns:
  Returns type of the item as number.

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubItem.get_content
documentation:
Returns content of the item. Content should be of type 'str' (Python 2) or 'bytes' (Python 3)

:Args:
  - default: Default value for the content if it is not already defined.

:Returns:
  Returns content of the item.

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubItem.set_content
documentation:
Sets content value for this item.

:Args:
  - content: Content value
Base class for the items in a book.
Items

classes.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubCover
documentation: Represents Cover image in the EPUB file.

classes.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubHtml

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubHtml.is_chapter
documentation:
Returns if this document is chapter or not.

:Returns:
  Returns book value.

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubHtml.get_type
documentation:
Always returns ebooklib.ITEM_DOCUMENT as type of this document.

:Returns:
  Always returns ebooklib.ITEM_DOCUMENT

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubHtml.set_language
documentation:
Sets language for this book item. By default it will use language of the book but it
can be overwritten with this call.

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubHtml.get_language
documentation:
Get language code for this book item. Language of the book item can be different from
the language settings defined globaly for book.

:Returns:
  As string returns language code.

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubHtml.add_link
documentation:
Add additional link to the document. Links will be embeded only inside of this document.

```pycon
>>> add_link(href='styles.css', rel='stylesheet', type='text/css')
```

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubHtml.get_links
documentation:
Returns list of additional links defined for this document.

:Returns:
  As tuple return list of links.

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubHtml.get_links_of_type
documentation:
Returns list of additional links of specific type.

:Returns:
  As tuple returns list of links.

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubHtml.add_item
documentation:
Add other item to this document. It will create additional links according to the item type.

:Args:
  - item: item we want to add defined as instance of EpubItem

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubHtml.get_body_content
documentation:
Returns content of BODY element for this HTML document. Content will be of type 'str' (Python 2)
or 'bytes' (Python 3).

:Returns:
  Returns content of this document.

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubHtml.get_content
documentation:
Returns content for this document as HTML string. Content will be of type 'str' (Python 2)
or 'bytes' (Python 3).

:Args:
  - default: Default value for the content if it is not defined.

:Returns:
  Returns content of this document.
Represents HTML document in the EPUB file.

classes.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubCoverHtml

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubCoverHtml.is_chapter
documentation:
Returns if this document is chapter or not.

:Returns:
  Returns book value.

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubCoverHtml.get_content
documentation:
Returns content for cover page as HTML string. Content will be of type 'str' (Python 2) or 'bytes' (Python 3).

:Returns:
  Returns content of this document.
Represents Cover page in the EPUB file.

classes.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubNav

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubNav.is_chapter
documentation:
Returns if this document is chapter or not.

:Returns:
  Returns book value.
Represents Navigation Document in the EPUB file.

classes.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubImage
documentation: Represents Image in the EPUB file.

classes.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubBook

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubBook.set_identifier
documentation:
Sets unique id for this epub

:Args:
  - uid: Value of unique identifier for this book

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubBook.set_title
documentation:
Set title. You can set multiple titles.

:Args:
  - title: Title value

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubBook.set_language
documentation:
Set language for this epub. You can set multiple languages. Specific items in the book can have
different language settings.

:Args:
  - lang: Language code

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubBook.set_direction
documentation:
:Args:
  - direction: Options are "ltr", "rtl" and "default"

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubBook.set_cover
documentation:
Set cover and create cover document if needed.

:Args:
  - file_name: file name of the cover page
  - content: Content for the cover image
  - create_page: Should cover page be defined. Defined as bool value (optional). Default value is True.

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubBook.add_item
documentation:
Add additional item to the book. If not defined, media type and chapter id will be defined
for the item.

:Args:
  - item: EpubItem instance

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubBook.get_item_with_id
documentation:
Returns item for defined UID.

```pycon
>>> book.get_item_with_id('image_001')
```

```
Args

uid: UID for the item

Returns

Returns item object. Returns None if nothing was found.
```

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubBook.get_item_with_href
documentation:
Returns item for defined HREF.

```pycon
>>> book.get_item_with_href('EPUB/document.xhtml')
```

```
Args

href: HREF for the item we are searching for

Returns

Returns item object. Returns None if nothing was found.
```

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubBook.get_items
documentation:
Returns all items attached to this book.

:Returns:
  Returns all items as tuple.

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubBook.get_items_of_type
documentation:
Returns all items of specified type.

```pycon
>>> book.get_items_of_type(epub.ITEM_IMAGE)
```

```
Args

item_type: Type for items we are searching for

Returns

Returns found items as tuple.
```

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubBook.get_items_of_media_type
documentation:
Returns all items of specified media type.

:Args:
  - media_type: Media type for items we are searching for

:Returns:
  Returns found items as tuple.

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubBook.set_template
documentation:
Defines templates which are used to generate certain types of pages. When defining new value for the template
we have to use content of type 'str' (Python 2) or 'bytes' (Python 3).

At the moment we use these templates:
  - ncx
  - nav
  - chapter
  - cover

:Args:
  - name: Name for the template
  - value: Content for the template

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubBook.get_template
documentation:
Returns value for the template.

:Args:
  - name: template name

:Returns:
  Value of the template.

methods.name: ebook2text.ebooklib.epub.EpubBook.add_prefix
documentation:
Appends custom prefix to be added to the content.opf document

```pycon
>>> epub_book.add_prefix('bkterms', 'http://booktype.org/')
```

```
Args

name: namespave name
- uri: URI for the namespace
```
EpubBook
